rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER YANET CALLES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. First Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien And For Judgment That No Lien Exists, And For Costs And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2023 line 2. PETITIONER PARAMOUNT ESTATE LLC'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN AND FOR JUDGMENT THAT NO LIEN EXISTS, AND FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEYS FEES is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CC 8486(b) and CC 8116(c). Additionally, it does not appear from the service reports filed on January 17, 2023 that the person subserved is a member of the household at 11 Humboldt and may be an occupant of 15 Humboldt. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL WILLIAMS VS. AYANA JOHNSON, AS TRUSTEE OF THE AURORA WILLIAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT DECEASED PLAINTIFFS LONG-TERM SPOUSE CAROLYN WILLIAMS AS HIS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST IN THIS ACTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2023 line 6. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT DECEASED PLAINTIFFS LONG-TERM SPOUSE CAROLYN WILLIAMS AS HIS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST IN THIS ACTION is continued to April 21, 2023. Successor-in-interest to provide a copy of the title/probate order, etc. showing that she is the new legal owner of 50% interest in the property by no later than April 14, 2023. Alternatively, successor-in-interest may file a supplemental brief no later than April 14, 2023, not to exceed 2 pages, limited to citations to authority permitting an heir to proceed as a party in a partition action. If no such authorities may be founded, supplemental brief shall state so. Brief shall not include any citations to authorities that do not involve partition actions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2023 line 8. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER JA VS. BENSON WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANTS BENSON WONG AND CATHI WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice complies with state and local laws regarding the proposed date of termination. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. No authority is cited for the proposition that omission of the express allegation regarding continued possession without permission, renders the complaint defective. (Motion 3: 12-19). Hearing will be in department 318 at 9:30 a.m. =(318/CCH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 1140 0818; Password: 976491; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 1140 0818; Password: 976491). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department318@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
